# **Unified Wire & Cable: New Website Proposal**

As an agency specializing in best-practice digital marketing for the industrial sector, we understand that your website needs to be more than a digital brochure. It must be a powerful engine for building trust, showcasing your capabilities, and seamlessly delivering technical data to engineers and buyers.

Based on our deep analysis of eight leading competitors, we have engineered an approach to actively match and beat the competition. We will deliver a modern, comprehensive new website on Squarespace in time for the upcoming May tradeshow.

## **1\. Strategic Site Architecture**

We recommend a streamlined, 7-key landing page structure designed to provide users with multiple, highly logical paths to the products and information they need. Note that some elements from our initial research have been explicitly excluded per our discussions to keep the site focused and streamlined.

* **Home:** Highlighting featured and new products, latest innovations, and quick links to find a rep or download the product catalog.  
* **Products (Mega-Menu):** An advanced product navigation system categorized by material (Copper, Aluminum) and product type (Building Wire, Armored Cable, etc.) to help visitors quickly find what they are searching for.  
* **Markets & Industries:** Dedicated pathways for commercial construction, industrial, utility, data centers, renewable energy, and automotive/marine sectors.  
* **Resources & Engineering Tools:** Competitors like Republic Wire and Sequel Wire dedicate heavy sitemap real estate to compliance certificates and spec sheets. We will build a robust, publicly accessible document portal featuring a searchable Specification Library and Certificates of Compliance. Making these highly visible is crucial for building trust with electrical engineers and municipal buyers.  
* **Services & Capabilities:** Highlighting custom cable manufacturing, copper drawing, and supply chain logistics.  
* **About Us:** Showcasing the company's story, quality policies, sustainability efforts, and news.  
* **Contact Us & Support:** Featuring a rep locator map, general contact form, and directory for sales and engineering support.

## **2\. Key Innovations & Technological Edge**

To give Unified Wire & Cable a distinct operational advantage, we are integrating cutting-edge features:

* **Dynamic, Auto-Updating Spec Sheets:** We have concepted a web page spec sheet system driven by an editable Google Sheet. When you update a detail on the sheet, the web page updates automatically within 5 minutes, allowing visitors to download an instantly updated PDF without requiring any other software.  
* **AI-Enhanced Efficiency with Human Authenticity:** We will utilize AI tools for coding, advanced navigation, graphics, and video editing to drastically decrease costs and increase deployment speed. However, we will enforce massive human oversight to ensure nothing comes across as fake or inauthentic to your visitors.  
* **Proactive Email Marketing:** We will set up an email campaign to announce the new site, providing a low-cost, high-impact method to keep Unified top of mind on a regular basis.

## **3\. Brand Modernization**

The current "UWC" logo carries a strong mid-2000s "Web 2.0" aesthetic. The high-contrast metallic gradients and glossy sheen make it feel dated in today's more minimalist design landscape and harder to read at small sizes. We recommend a logo refresh focusing on simplification:

* **Option A ("Flat-Plus"):** Keep the bold, italicized structure but remove the 3D effects, opting for solid, high-impact colors to make the logo versatile for digital screens.  
* **Option B (Geometry & Negative Space):** Integrate the current diagonal slash more meaningfully into the typography to create a cohesive, "locked-in" mark.  
* **Option C (Contemporary Typography):** Switch to a slightly more open geometric sans-serif font and reduce the italics to make the brand feel more stable and modern.

## **4\. Timeline & Milestones**

To ensure the website is fully launched and optimized in time for your May tradeshow, we will work on the following accelerated timeline. We will meet weekly over Zoom to ensure alignment at every step.

* **Late February | Phase 1: Strategy, Architecture & Immediate Media Capture**  
  * Finalize site structure and approve the logo modernization route.  
  * Schedule and execute the on-site plant and people photography using a current iPhone model, setting a standard your staff can mimic later.  
  * Capture the 360-degree plant operations and record the natural, 7-minute staff video interviews.  
* **March | Phase 2: Design & Web Development**  
  * Build out the Squarespace platform and use the newly captured photography to visually build out the 7 key landing pages.  
  * Engineer the dynamic Google Sheet-to-PDF spec sheet infrastructure and the advanced product navigation.  
* **Early to Mid-April | Phase 3: Video Integration & Refinement**  
  * Finalize the edits for the 7-minute staff video interviews and embed the 360-degree plant capture into the site to bring out the human aspect of the organization.  
* **Late April | Phase 4: Launch Preparation**  
  * Apply massive human oversight and QA to all AI-assisted generation and development.  
  * Stage the initial email announcement campaign.  
* **First Week of May | Milestone 5: Go-Live**  
  * The website officially launches ahead of the May tradeshow.

## **5\. Project Investment**

To provide Unified Wire & Cable with an enterprise-level digital presence that rivals the largest players in your space, we leverage advanced AI tools to drastically increase efficiencies. This allows us to deliver top-tier functionality at a price point closer to your mid-size competitors.

The total investment for the initial website build is **$25,000**.

**Competitor Cost Context:** Enterprise sites like Southwire and Encore Wire cost between $75K to $200K+. Mid-size competitors like Lake Cable and Alan Wire paid between $20K and $60K for their builds. Our $25,000 build delivers enterprise capabilities for a mid-market investment.

## **6\. Ongoing Support & Post-Live Maintenance**

A website is a living asset. Following the launch, we provide **one month of free support** to ensure everything runs flawlessly.

**Platform Hosting:** The site will be hosted on Squarespace. Unified Wire will enter a credit card directly into Squarespace to pay $23/month (billed annually). This covers your core hosting, basic Squarespace email/chat support, and ongoing platform security upgrades.

**Prepaid Agency Support Packages:** After the first free month, you have the option to purchase a prepaid maintenance package for ongoing agency support, design updates, or marketing enhancements.

We carefully log hours against your chosen budget and provide monthly reports on your usage. We will automatically notify you when your budget is close to being fully used. The larger the package, the heavier the discount on our hourly rate:

* **4 hours:** $600 *(billed at $150/hr)*  
* **8 hours:** $1,000 *(billed at $125/hr)*  
* **13 hours:** $1,500 *(billed at $115/hr)*  
* **20 hours:** $2,000 *(billed at $100/hr)*  
* **27 hours:** $2,500 *(billed at $93/hr)*  
* **34 hours:** $3,000 *(billed at $88/hr)*  
* **50 hours:** $4,000 *(billed at $80/hr)*  
* **67 hours:** $5,000 *(billed at $75/hr)*
